context.summary: Turn your BBC micro:bit into a water bottle alert, so you can tell if someone else has drunk from it. You will learn how to use the micro:bit's LED display and accelerometer.
how_it_works: The micro:bit shows a heart on its LED display when your water bottle is upright.

The LED display shows a warning cross when a tilt is detected by the micro:bitâs accelerometer. The cross stays there until you reset the program by pressing the reset button on the back of the micro:bit or by disconnecting and reconnecting the battery.
An accelerometer measures forces in three dimensions, including gravity, so your projects can tell which way up your micro:bit is.
improve_it: Find a way to reset the program without using the micro:bitâs reset button, for example by pressing button A or B to show a heart icon.
Add an audible alarm using blocks from the music section of the MakeCode editor.
